parsed_review.summary: CP00-579 is a descriptor-only RP18 closeout pack (150 units, RP18.P07.M06.S01-RP18.P08.M02.S04, risk C). Read-tool inspection of the actual source (registry.js, service.js, validators.js, test/model.test.js, contract JSON, and the contract validator script) confirms the pack-level claim is honest and fully bounded: manifest declares production_ready:false, runtime_ready:false, implementation_layer:"descriptor". Scope and deliverable/micro-title distributions match exactly across prompt, baseline, CP579_REQUIREMENTS, manifest, and contract, and the validator enforces every drift check. Descriptor-only/no-write/no-real-data boundaries are stamped by freezeCp579Result and the per-row factory and inherited through the intact CP551->CP579 no-write attestation chain. Claude remains a read-only reviewer (claude_is_final_approval:false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false, allowed tools Read/Grep/Glob); enterprise trust is not claimed from local validation. Hermes is used only for descriptor review packet/receipt handling (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false, receipt_shape ..._no_write), not product runtime. CP178+ hardening blockers (auth failure, zero-byte/malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, missing references) are present in forbidden_review_evidence. Readiness auth preflight passed with valid JSON and no prior invalid attempts. The Hermes production_ready_candidate:true is correctly scoped to descriptor coverage+slice validity, not runtime/enterprise readiness. Unrelated dirty files are correctly segregated. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings; final runtime/enterprise-trust approval remains human-gated as designed.
